benchmark_aplicado: Servicio local (salud, legal) — SEO 55, UX/UI 60, Marketing 60, Contenido 65, Técnico 50. Applied because Carolina Dental Alliance is a multi-location local dental healthcare provider in South Carolina, fitting the local health service category.

positivos: Meta title and description present with geographic keywords (South Carolina, Spartanburg, Greenville, Columbia)
Clear navigation structure with logical service hierarchy and 13 menu items
Multiple CTAs with descriptive text (Choose Your Location, Contact Us, New Patients)
negativos: H1 is generic 'Welcome to Carolina Dental Alliance' — lacks primary keyword like 'dentist' or 'dental care'
No meta keywords defined
Most images use base64 lazy-loading placeholders with missing or generic alt text
Meta description is thin and generic — doesn't mention services or unique value proposition
No structured heading hierarchy visible (H1 > H2 > H3 progression unclear)

recomendaciones.accion: Rewrite H1 to include primary keyword: 'Compassionate Dental Care Across South Carolina'
Optimize meta description to 150-160 chars including services, locations, and CTA
Add descriptive alt text to all images with service-related keywords
Implement proper H1>H2>H3 heading hierarchy across all sections

positivos: Consistent color palette with clear primary (#4fbbda) and secondary (#2c659c) brand colors
Two complementary font families (Work Sans for headings, Lato for body) create visual hierarchy
Service cards provide scannable grid layout for key offerings
negativos: Hero section has minimal visual impact — white overlay at 50% opacity with basic heading
Location section lists 17 offices as plain text without visual differentiation or map integration
Too many color values detected (8+) suggesting inconsistent application across the site
No visible social proof elements (testimonials, ratings) above the fold
Services list is plain comma-separated text instead of visual cards or icons

recomendaciones.accion: Redesign hero with compelling imagery, clear value proposition, and prominent CTA button
Convert locations section to interactive card grid with address, phone, and map link per office
Reduce color palette to 3 primary + 2 neutral for consistency
Add icons to services list for scannability and visual engagement

positivos: Clear value proposition mentioning 17 locations across South Carolina — emphasizes convenience
Special offer prominently displayed (braces starting at $3,995 with free consultation)
Multiple pathways for different user intents (New Patients, Services, Dental Plan, Offers)
negativos: No social proof — zero testimonials, reviews, patient counts, or trust badges visible
No urgency or scarcity elements in CTAs or offers
Hero CTA 'Choose Your Location' is navigation-focused, not benefit-focused
No differentiation from competitors — messaging is generic dental language
Broken social media links (hash placeholders instead of actual URLs)

recomendaciones.accion: Add patient testimonials section with star ratings and real quotes
Change hero CTA to benefit-focused: 'Book Your Free Consultation' or 'Find Your Dentist'
Add trust indicators: years in business, number of patients served, Google review rating
Fix social media links with actual profile URLs

positivos: About section contains detailed, specific copy about the practice's values and approach
Comprehensive services list covering 9+ dental specialties
17 locations listed — demonstrates scale and accessibility
negativos: Card descriptions are generic and interchangeable — not specific to each service
No FAQ section addressing common patient concerns (cost, insurance, pain, first visit)
Email address missing from contact information
Operating hours not listed anywhere on the homepage
No individual dentist bios or team information visible

recomendaciones.accion: Add FAQ section addressing insurance, first visit process, emergency care, and costs
Rewrite service card descriptions with specific benefits and outcomes for each
Add email and operating hours to contact information
Create 'Meet Our Team' section with dentist photos and credentials

positivos: WordPress CMS with established plugin ecosystem (Gravity Forms, Schema.org)
Triple analytics setup (UA, GTM, GA4) for comprehensive tracking
Responsive design with hamburger menu for mobile
negativos: WordPress with Material Design theme — heavy and potentially slow for a primarily informational site
Images using base64 lazy-loading suggests performance optimization issues
Legacy Universal Analytics (UA-50584001-1) still present alongside GA4
Gravity Forms detected but form fields not visible — possibly broken or hidden

recomendaciones.accion: Migrate to lightweight static site (HTML + Tailwind) for faster load times
Remove legacy Universal Analytics tracking code
Optimize image delivery — use WebP format with proper srcset for responsive images
Verify Gravity Forms contact form is functional and visible to users

tecnico.stack_actual: WordPress, Material Design theme, Gravity Forms, Google Analytics/GTM/GA4, Schema.org
tecnico.stack_recomendado: HTML + Tailwind CSS + Alpine.js — lightweight, fast-loading static site appropriate for multi-location dental practice

quick_wins: Rewrite H1 to include primary dental keyword and location
Add patient testimonials with star ratings to homepage
Change hero CTA from 'Choose Your Location' to 'Book Your Free Consultation'
Add trust badges: years in business, patients served, Google rating
Add email and operating hours to contact section

semana_1: Rewrite H1 and meta description with targeted keywords
Change hero CTA to benefit-focused language
Add trust indicators and social proof numbers
Add email and operating hours to contact info
mes_1: Redesign hero section with compelling imagery and clear value proposition
Convert locations to interactive card grid with details per office
Add FAQ section for common dental concerns
Implement proper heading hierarchy and image alt text optimization
largo_plazo: Migrate to lightweight static site for performance
Create Meet Our Team section with dentist bios
Build individual location pages with unique content and local SEO
Remove legacy analytics and optimize image delivery pipeline

resumen_ejecutivo: Carolina Dental Alliance has a functional but underperforming website that scores 43/100, well below the 58-benchmark for local health services — the homepage lacks social proof, has a generic hero, and misses critical trust signals that dental patients need. The biggest opportunity is adding testimonials, trust badges, and a benefit-focused CTA to the hero, which could dramatically improve conversion from the site's existing traffic across 17 locations. Immediate next step: rebuild the homepage with a modern, lightweight stack focusing on social proof, clear service differentiation, and location-specific CTAs.

stack_para_web.justificacion: Medium complexity multi-location dental practice with 17 offices needs a performant static site with component reuse for location cards. Astro provides excellent static generation with partial hydration for interactive elements like location finder, while Tailwind ensures consistent design system across all sections.
